source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Palpitation – 14
source.population: Outpatients presenting with palpitations; no precise age range is stated in the applicability section.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth palpitation documentation; partial for a setting-agnostic GP workflow.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Palpitation – 14

items.rationale: Relevant to clinician-recorded onset, duration, pattern, triggers, associated features, medicines and history without identifying rhythm.

items.final_wording: Lists chest pain, dizziness or syncope, breathlessness, focal neurology, dehydration, family sudden death, exertional onset, systemic causes, and hypotension.

items.rationale: Relevant to concern features actually assessed; absence cannot be presumed.

items.final_wording: Lists ECG, ambulatory monitoring, and blood tests selected from the clinical history.
